accurate.why: Accurately describes the conveyance of approximately 77 acres across five parcels to Frontier and Hitchcock Counties, the 3-year negotiation deadline, 2-year code compliance requirement, fair market value payment, reconveyance restrictions, flood liability disclaimer, indemnification requirement, and the fallback provision if no agreement is reached.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims. Total acreage (~77.2 acres) and all other figures are accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose: conveying federal reclamation lands at Swanson and Hugh Butler Reservoirs to the Nebraska counties.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Describes the conveyance from federal to county ownership, the change in land management authority.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes reconveyance restrictions (only to NE public/governmental entity), existing rights, flood liability disclaimer, indemnification requirement, and land must be conveyed whole.

effective_dates.why: Notes 3-year negotiation deadline, 2-year code compliance deadline, and Dec 23, 2024 approval date.

major_provisions_covered.why: All key operative provisions covered including the five parcels, payment terms, management requirements, existing agreements, and fallback.

correct_entities.why: Bureau of Reclamation, Secretary of Interior, Hitchcock County, Frontier County, Nebraska, Swanson Reservoir, Hugh Butler Reservoir, John D. Dingell Jr. Act all correctly referenced.

correct_figures.why: ~77 acres total, 3-year deadline, 2-year compliance, Dec 23, 2024 all accurate.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
